description: Task 2: Autonomous enforcement quality over 72-hour AASE simulations (N = 700 applications: 500 benign / 200 malicious; delta = 60 s windows, 4,320 steps). Final values as reported in the paper (tab:task2). mean +/- std over seeds {7, 42, 123, 777, 2024}. AIPR/EPR/AET-R are model-independent ground-truth outcomes; PRR is TrustGuard's internal risk score (comparability only); FIR is a cost-weighted diagnostic, not a trained constraint. Latency is the median seconds from anomaly onset to first enforcement.

protocol.injection: log-normal-rate Poisson process, median 3.1 anomalous invocations/hour, 40/60 foreground/background

Android Static Policy.note: install-time policy; cannot enforce at runtime

key_findings: TrustGuard blocks 63.4% of ground-truth anomalous invocations and prevents 71.8% of taint-verified exfiltration flows at 2.1% FRR (within the 2.5% budget).
vs. MAPPO-Lagrangian (B8): +5.1 AIPR / +5.8 EPR at equal constraint satisfaction — isolates the role-specialized architecture.
vs. constrained single agent (B7): +13.6 AIPR at essentially the same FRR — the properly controlled multi-agent comparison.
For every FRR-constrained method FIR sits roughly 2x its FRR; FRR alone understates the false-intervention burden roughly twofold.
